reason: Response A has the structured, thorough, and somewhat encyclopedic quality typical of Claude Haiku — it methodically covers each factor (lighting, color, clutter, sound, temperature, texture, spatial arrangement) in organized paragraphs without strong stylistic flourishes. Response B has a more distinctive, opinionated voice with rhetorical devices ("wins almost every time," "asks nothing of you visually," "None of this is complicated") and a punchy, essay-like closing paragraph that feels more like a deliberate stylistic imitation than Haiku's default tone. Haiku tends toward comprehensive, helpful explanations rather than aphoristic prose.
